name: Eliopulos Stew
recipeIngredient: 3/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon, or to taste
4 1/4 pounds lean beef, cut into 1-inch cubes
salt and freshly ground pepper -- to taste
1/2 pound Muenster or jack cheese, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, flameproof casserole over medium heat, heat the oil. Sauté onions in the oil over medium heat until soft, about 5 minutes.
2. Break up the tomatoes into small pieces. Stir together the tomatoes and their juice, wine, tomato paste, brown sugar, currants, bay leaves, cinnamon, cumin, and cloves; add to the casserole.
3. Add beef and simmer, covered, about 2 hours, or until meat is fork-tender. Season to taste with salt and pepper, and adjust other seasonings as needed. If the stew is too liquid, uncover the pan for the last 30 minutes of cooking so that excess liquid will evaporate.
4. To serve immediately: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Remove the casserole from heat and scatter cheese and nuts over top. Bake at 350 degrees F until cheese melts (about 5 minutes).
